TATA POWER: COMMISSIONS 100.8 MW JEWALI WIND PROJECT IN MAHARASHTRA 🌬️⚡

• 🌬️ Tata Power Renewable Energy Ltd. (TPREL) has commissioned its 100.8 MW Jewali Wind Project in Dharashiv, Maharashtra

• ⚡ The project is expected to generate 299 million units of clean electricity annually and offset over 245 million kg of CO₂ emissions every year

• 🏙️ The power generated will be supplied to Tata Power Mumbai Distribution, supporting its Renewable Purchase Obligation (RPO) compliance

• 📈 With this commissioning, TPREL's total renewable utility capacity has reached 11.6 GW, including 6.7 GW operational and 4.9 GW under implementation

• 🎯 The project strengthens Tata Power's renewable portfolio and supports its goal of achieving 100% clean energy generation by 2045 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The commissioning expands Tata Power's renewable capacity, enhances clean energy generation and strengthens long-term growth visibility.)

LORD'S MARK INDUSTRIES: ACQUIRES 30% STAKE IN AIVITA TO EXPAND ROBOTIC SURGERY BUSINESS 🤝🏥

• 🤝 Acquired a 30% equity stake in Aivita Private Limited, a healthcare technology company focused on robotic surgery solutions

• 🤖 Aivita is building India's execution and intelligence layer for robotic surgery, aimed at improving surgical precision, training and hospital collaboration

• 💰 The company acquired 30,000 equity shares for ₹3 lakh through cash consideration, making Aivita an associate company

• 🏥 The acquisition strengthens Lord's Mark's healthcare portfolio spanning diagnostics, medical devices, AI-enabled healthcare solutions and indigenous healthcare manufacturing

• 🚀 The investment is expected to accelerate deployment of robotic surgical platforms and support technology-led healthcare infrastructure across India 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The strategic acquisition strengthens Lord's Mark's presence in advanced healthcare technology and opens new long-term growth opportunities in robotic surgery.)

TATA POWER: COMMISSIONS 100.8 MW JEWALI WIND PROJECT IN MAHARASHTRA 🌬️⚡

• 🌬️ Tata Power Renewable Energy Ltd. (TPREL) has commissioned its 100.8 MW Jewali Wind Project in Dharashiv, Maharashtra

IIFL FINANCE: S&P & MOODY'S ASSIGN RATINGS TO $300 MN NOTES 🌍💵

• ⭐ S&P Global Ratings assigned a B+ rating to IIFL Finance's proposed USD 300 million 7.60% Senior Secured Fixed Rate Notes due 2030

• ⭐ Moody's Ratings assigned a Ba3 rating to the proposed notes under the company's USD 1.5 billion Global Medium Term Note (GMTN) Programme

• 💰 The proposed issue is part of IIFL Finance's USD 1.5 billion GMTN Programme

• 🌱 The proceeds will be used in line with the company's Social Financing Framework to support lending growth and comply with India's ECB regulations

• 📊 Moody's maintained a Stable Outlook, citing IIFL Finance's strong retail lending franchise, diversified funding profile and increasing shift towards secured lending 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The international credit ratings support the proposed overseas bond issuance and enhance IIFL Finance's access to global funding markets.)

JASH ENGINEERING: TARGETS ₹1,500 CR REVENUE BY FY31 🌍🚀

• 🎯 Company targets ₹1,500 crore+ revenue by FY31, with ₹1,200 crore capacity after ongoing expansions

• 🏭 Plans to set up manufacturing plants in Saudi Arabia and Houston (USA) by FY28

• 📈 Targets around 19% consolidated revenue growth in FY27, backed by an order book of ₹899 crore (as of June 27, 2026)

• 🇺🇸 Expansion strategy focuses on strengthening execution in the US, Middle East and MENA markets

🟢 Impact: Positive (Expansion plans, healthy order book and strong FY27/FY31 growth targets support long-term growth outlook.)

MAX ESTATES: Q1 PRE-SALES SURGE 5X TO ₹1,100 CR 🏡📈

• 🏘️ Q1 FY27 pre-sales surged 5x+ YoY to ~₹1,100 crore, with 487 units sold compared to 43 units in Q1 FY26

• 🌆 Phase 1 of The Terraces was fully sold, generating ~₹500 crore in sales, while sustenance sales contributed ~₹600 crore

• 💰 Collections during Q1 FY27 stood at ~₹500 crore

• 🚀 The company has a GDV pipeline of over ₹17,200 crore, with major residential launches planned in Q2 and Q3 FY27

• 🏢 The commercial portfolio remains 100% leased, generating ₹150+ crore annual rental income, with potential to reach ₹700+ crore over the next five years

IDFC FIRST BANK: Q1 LOAN BOOK UP 21%, CASA DEPOSITS RISE 25% 🏦📈

• 💰 Loans & Advances grew 20.6% YoY and 5.2% QoQ to ₹3,05,488 crore

• 💵 Total Deposits increased 17.7% YoY and 5.9% QoQ to ₹3,11,874 crore

• 🏦 CASA Deposits surged 24.7% YoY and 8.1% QoQ to ₹1,58,563 crore, with CASA ratio improving to 50.8% from 48.0% a year ago

• 📊 Credit-Deposit Ratio stood at 95.5%, compared with 93.4% a year earlier

• ✅ The bank said asset quality continues to improve compared to prior periods

RAYMOND REALTY: Q1 PRE-SALES JUMP 129%, COLLECTIONS UP 47%

•  Q1 FY27 pre-sales surged 129% YoY to ₹700 crore, despite no new residential project launches during the quarter

•  Collections increased 47% YoY to ₹550 crore, reflecting strong cash flow and healthy customer demand

•  Outstanding borrowings stood at ₹1,097 crore as of 30 June 2026, primarily to fund construction and working capital for 7 projects launched in FY26

•  The company maintained a Net Debt/Equity ratio below 1.0x and reiterated its FY27 EBITDA margin guidance of 17–19%

• Management said strong demand across its MMR residential portfolio and robust collections position the company for continued growth

CAPITALNUMBERS INFOTECH: SIGNS AGREEMENT TO ACQUIRE EPITOME CLOUD FOR ~₹40 CR 🌍🤝

• 🤝 Signed a Stock Purchase Agreement (SPA) to acquire 100% ownership of Epitome Cloud Inc. (USA) and its Indian subsidiary Epitome Cloud Technology Pvt. Ltd.

• 💰 The acquisition is valued at approximately ₹40 crore (subject to net working capital and other agreed adjustments) through cash consideration

• ☁️ Epitome Cloud specializes in Salesforce consulting, CPQ, Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM), enterprise workflow automation and digital transformation services

• 🇺🇸 The acquisition is expected to strengthen CapitalNumbers' Salesforce capabilities, expand its US market presence, add specialized delivery expertise and create cross-selling opportunities

• ⏳ The transaction is expected to be completed in 8–12 weeks, subject to customary conditions, and is not a related-party transaction 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The acquisition expands CapitalNumbers' global capabilities, strengthens its presence in the US market and enhances long-term growth opportunities.)

KP ENERGY: KP GROUP APPOINTS PROF. SUNIL KUMAR MAHESHWARI AS VICE-CHAIRMAN 👨‍💼🌱

• 👨‍💼 Prof. Sunil Kumar Maheshwari has been appointed as Vice-Chairman of KP Group and has joined the Boards of KP Energy, KPI Green Energy and KP Green Engineering

• 🎓 He brings nearly 40 years of experience across academia, governance, strategy, restructuring and the power & energy sector, and is an alumnus of IIT Delhi and IIM Ahmedabad

• 🏢 He has served on the boards of UCO Bank, Andhra Bank and NTPC School of Business, and previously held senior academic and advisory roles, including at IIM Ahmedabad

• 🌍 The appointment is aimed at strengthening KP Group's leadership as it continues expanding its renewable energy and infrastructure businesses

• 📈 Management believes his expertise in governance, strategy and organizational transformation will help drive the group's next phase of sustainable growth 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The appointment strengthens leadership and corporate governance, supporting KP Energy's long-term growth strategy.)

NIIT: INTEGRATES STACKROUTE & RPS CONSULTING INTO ENTERPRISE TECH BUSINESS 🤝💻

• 🚀 Integrated StackRoute and RPS Consulting into a unified Enterprise Tech business to strengthen enterprise technology skilling and workforce transformation

• 👩‍💼 Appointed Dr. Vishnupriya Raghavan to lead the combined Enterprise Tech business

• 🤖 The integrated platform will focus on AI-first engineering, AI-led SRE, cloud transformation, cybersecurity, data engineering, enterprise architecture and technology leadership

• 🌍 The move combines StackRoute's engineering expertise with RPS Consulting's OEM partnerships to deliver end-to-end enterprise capability building at scale

• 📈 The integration is aimed at helping enterprises build future-ready technology talent, accelerate digital transformation and strengthen NIIT's enterprise learning business 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The integration enhances NIIT's enterprise offerings, strengthens its technology skilling capabilities and supports long-term growth in digital transformation services.)

ORIENTAL RAIL INFRASTRUCTURE: FORMS JV LLP FOR RAILWAY COACH PROJECTS 🚆🤝

• 🤝 Incorporated ABOR Projects LLP as a joint venture with A B Composites Pvt. Ltd., with ORIL holding 49% and A B Composites 51%

• 🏗️ The LLP will undertake turnkey railway coach refurbishment projects and other allied activities in the railway sector

• 💰 ORIL will invest ₹4.90 lakh as its initial capital contribution for its 49% partnership interest

• 📅 The LLP received its Certificate of Incorporation on 2 July 2026 and is yet to commence operations

• 📈 The JV is expected to strengthen ORIL's presence in the railway sector, diversify its business portfolio and create opportunities for high-value turnkey projects 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The joint venture expands ORIL's presence in railway infrastructure and supports long-term revenue growth.)

RAYMOND REALTY | Q1 Pre-Sales Jump 129% YoY

Key Highlights
- Pre-sales up 129% YoY
- Collections rose 47% YoY
- No new project launches
- EBITDA guidance maintained

Business Updates
- Q1 pre-sales at ₹700 crore
- Collections reached ₹550 crore
- Net Debt-to-Equity below 1.0x
- Strong demand across MMR projects

Key Takeaway
- Raymond Realty delivered strong Q1 FY27 pre-sales and collections despite no new launches, reflecting healthy demand across its MMR portfolio, while maintaining a comfortable leverage profile and reaffirming its FY27 EBITDA margin guidance of 17–19%.

Alkali Metals – ICRA Revises Outlook to Stable

 ICRA has reaffirmed the credit ratings for Alkali Metals Ltd.'s bank facilities.

 The ₹18 crore long-term fund-based facilities have been reaffirmed at [ICRA]BB+, with the outlook revised from Negative to Stable.

 The ₹7 crore short-term non-fund-based facilities have been reaffirmed at [ICRA]A4+.

 The company's total rated bank facilities stand at ₹25 crore.

SCHNEIDER ELECTRIC INFRASTRUCTURE: BOARD APPROVES HIGHER CAPEX FOR CAPACITY EXPANSION ⚡🏭

• 🏗️ Board approved increasing capex for the Kolkata Medium Voltage Components (KMVC) facility from ₹138 crore to ₹184 crore

• ⚡ The investment will expand Medium Voltage Vacuum Interrupter manufacturing capacity from 180,000 units to 250,000 units per annum by Q1 FY29

• 🔧 Board also increased capex for the Mechanism Assembly Line at KMVC from ₹90.6 crore to ₹107.2 crore to support future growth and exports

• 💰 The projects will be funded through internal accruals and/or borrowings

• 🌍 The expanded investment supports the company's manufacturing localisation strategy, export growth plans, capacity augmentation and business continuity objectives 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The higher capex strengthens manufacturing capacity, supports export growth and positions the company for higher future demand.)

ARVIND: BOARD APPROVES FUND RAISE OF UP TO ₹600 CR 💰📈

• 🏦 Board approved raising up to ₹600 crore through QIP or other permissible modes, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals

• 📄 The fund raise may include equity shares, convertible securities, preference shares, debentures, ADRs, GDRs, FCCBs or other eligible instruments, in one or more tranches

• 👨‍💼 The Board has authorised the Finance Committee to decide the timing, pricing and other terms of the issuance

• 🗳️ Approved the Postal Ballot process to seek shareholders' approval for the proposed fund raise

• 🚀 The proposed capital raise is intended to strengthen the company's financial flexibility and support future growth initiatives 

🟢 Impact: Neutral (While the fund raise enhances financial flexibility for future growth, it may also result in equity dilution depending on the mode and pricing of issuance.)

INDIAN HUME PIPE: BAGS ₹738.61 CR WATER SUPPLY PROJECT IN RAJASTHAN 💧📑

• 🏗️ Received a Letter of Acceptance (LoA) worth ₹738.61 crore (including GST) from the Public Health Engineering Department (PHED), Rajasthan

• 🚰 The contract involves design, construction and O&M of a Narmada-based water supply project covering 275 villages in Dhorimanna and Chouhtan blocks, Barmer district

• 🌍 The project is being executed under the JICA-funded Rajasthan Rural Water Supply & Fluorosis Mitigation Project – Phase II

• ⏳ The project is to be completed within 24 months, followed by 1-year defect notification period and 10 years of operation & maintenance

• 🤝 The order has been awarded by a domestic government entity and is not a related-party transaction 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The large government order significantly strengthens Indian Hume Pipe's order book and provides long-term revenue visibility through execution and O&M.)

MARSONS: BAGS ₹17.94 CR ORDER FOR POWER TRANSFORMERS ⚡📦

• 🤝 Received a purchase order worth ₹17.94 crore (including GST) from S.T. Electricals Pvt. Ltd.

• ⚡ The order is for the supply of 10 MVA power transformers

• ⏳ The contract is scheduled to be executed within 6 months

• 🇮🇳 The order has been awarded by a domestic entity and is not a related-party transaction

• 📈 The order strengthens Marsons' order book and enhances revenue visibility in the power equipment business 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The new order improves revenue visibility, strengthens the order book and supports business growth.)

ZYDUS LIFESCIENCES: SIGNS MOU WITH APOLLO HOSPITALS FOR MULTI-CANCER BLOOD TEST 🤝🩺

• 🤝 Signed an MoU with Apollo Hospitals to expand access to the Shield™ Multi-Cancer Detection (MCD) blood test across India

• 🩸 The methylation-based blood test can detect signals for 10 common cancers, including breast, lung, colorectal, liver and pancreatic cancer, through a single blood sample

• 🌍 Zydus holds an exclusive agreement with Guardant Health to make the Shield™ MCD test available in India

• 👥 The test is intended for individuals aged 45 years and above who are at average cancer risk, supporting earlier cancer detection

• 🚀 The partnership strengthens Zydus' precision oncology portfolio and expands access to advanced cancer screening solutions in India 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The collaboration enhances Zydus' oncology diagnostics portfolio and supports long-term growth in precision healthcare.)

EFFWA INFRA & RESEARCH: BAGS MAJOR JSW STEEL ORDER 🏗️📑

• 🤝 Secured a major work order from JSW Steel

• 🏭 The contract is for execution of industrial infrastructure / construction works for JSW Steel's project

• 📅 The order will be executed within the timeline specified in the contract

Powerica
Recently, there is lot of hype around anything related to data centers...

This is a newly listed stock, and there was certainly lot of hoopla around it as 15% of the orderbook is from data centers and fast growing.

Did some deep dive - 
It seems the company is more of a systems integrator + EPC business for gensets. It gets critical parts like engine, alternator etc. from Cummins and builds the gensets along with installation.

Also, all the major genset orders from hyperscalers go directly to Cummins. Only the small-scale desi data center or edge data center orders come to Powerica

Basically, its a low-tech low-margin business...nothing to get excited about.

The other business is wind power - IPP + EPC, which is having its own challenges.

Power Equipment Sector | Government Grants 2-Year PPO Exemption to Four Chinese Vendors

Key Highlights
- 2-year PPO exemption
- Four Chinese vendors
- PSU tender eligibility
- Transformer sector impact

Key Developments
- TBEA: EHV transformers & reactors
- Nanjing: GIS & HVDC insulators
- Northeast: 400/765 kV GIS
- Taikai: 400/765 kV GIS

Key Takeaway
- The government's two-year exemption from Public Procurement Order (PPO) restrictions allows four Chinese electrical equipment manufacturers to participate in PSU tenders for high-voltage transformers and GIS switchgear until domestic manufacturing capacity expands. The move could increase competitive intensity and moderate order opportunities for domestic players such as GE Vernova, CG Power, Siemens India and Hitachi Energy India, particularly in the high-voltage transmission equipment segment.

text:
HCLTECH: WINS US$1.14 BILLION AI-LED DIGITAL TRANSFORMATION DEAL 🤝💻

• 💰 Signed a strategic partnership worth US$1.14 billion with a Europe-headquartered Fortune Global 50 company

• 🤖 HCLTech will establish an AI-driven operating model to transform and manage the client's Global Digital Workplace and Enterprise Networks

• 📅 The agreement will run from July 2026 to December 2031, with an option to extend for another 5 years

• 🆕 The company stated that the contract is entirely new business for HCLTech

• 🌍 The deal further strengthens HCLTech's position in AI-led digital transformation and managed services 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The US$1.14 billion deal provides strong long-term revenue visibility and reinforces HCLTech's leadership in AI-driven enterprise transformation.)

Tata Steel expects €500 million EBITDA from its Dutch operations in FY27!
Chairman N Chandrasekaran said the Netherlands business is on track to deliver strong earnings this year, with a long-term goal of €800 million to €1 billion. Despite challenges like environmental rules in Europe, the company is confident about future growth.

SambhvSteel Q1 FY27 Business Update

🔹Highest-ever Q1 sales volume for Value Added Products at 101,191 tonnes (⬆️27% YoY)
🔹Pre-Galvanised (GP) Coils & Pipes: 29,814 tonnes (⬆️49% YoY)
🔹Stainless Steel Coils: 14,760 tonnes (⬆️56% YoY)
🔹Structural Pipes & Tubes: 56,617 tonnes (⬆️13% YoY)
🔹Total sales volume remained stable at 107,771 tonnes despite global disruptions

ANGEL ONE: Q1 CLIENT BASE UP 19%, ORDER VOLUME RISES 18% 📈📊

• 👥 Client base grew 18.8% YoY to 38.59 million, while Q1 gross client acquisition stood at 1.34 million

• 📈 Q1 order volume increased 18.4% YoY to 406.14 million, with average daily orders rising 20.3% YoY to 6.77 million

• 💰 Average client funding book reached a record ₹61.37 billion, up 45.9% YoY, reflecting continued growth in the margin funding business

• 🚀 Overall ADTO (option premium basis) surged 149.8% YoY, while commodity ADTO jumped 184.6% YoY

• 📊 The company said cash market share improved sequentially, while client engagement remained strong despite softer market conditions

IEX: Q1 POWER TRADED VOLUME RISES 16%, JUNE VOLUME UP 13% ⚡📈

• ⚡ Q1 FY27 electricity traded volume increased 15.9% YoY to 37.5 BU, while June 2026 volume rose 12.5% YoY to 12.2 BU

• 🔄 Real-Time Market (RTM) volume grew 23.5% YoY in Q1 FY27 to 16.0 BU, and 25.7% YoY in June to 5.4 BU

• 🌱 Green Market volume increased 6.3% YoY during Q1 FY27 to 2,827 MU

• 📈 Day-Ahead Market (DAM) clearing price rose to ₹5.1/unit in Q1 FY27 (+15.7% YoY), while June DAM price increased to ₹5.2/unit (+32.4% YoY)

• ☀️ Higher electricity demand due to heatwaves and a delayed monsoon supported higher traded volumes and market prices during the quarter

JAPANESE STOCKS SEE BIGGEST FOREIGN OUTFLOW SINCE MARCH 🇯🇵📉

• 💸 Foreign investors sold ¥1.82 trillion ($11.2 billion) worth of Japanese stocks last week, the biggest weekly outflow since March 28

• 📉 Foreign investors also sold ¥493.7 billion of long-term bonds and ¥2.43 trillion of short-term bills

• 🛍️ Japanese retail investors stepped in with record share purchases to cushion the market selloff

• ⚠️ Selling comes as the recent technology-driven rally in Japanese equities loses momentum

🔴 Impact: Negative for Japanese equities in the near term; limited direct impact on Indian markets, though global risk sentiment could remain cautious.

Global Semiconductor Supply Chain Remains Highly Concentrated

Key Highlights:

🏭 TSMC controls over 80% of the world's leading-edge (<16nm) chip production, powering most AI servers and premium smartphones.

🌏 Taiwan, South Korea, and China account for ~85% of global semiconductor foundry revenue, highlighting regional concentration.

💾 Samsung and SK Hynix produce ~70% of the world's DRAM memory chips.

⚙️ ASML holds a near-100% monopoly in EUV lithography, the critical technology required to manufacture chips below 7nm.

🇺🇸 US companies generate around 60% of global chip design revenue, while China accounts for ~38% of global OSAT (chip packaging and testing) capacity.

🏗️ 56% of global wafer capacity still lies in mature nodes (≥34nm), creating an opportunity for emerging semiconductor manufacturing hubs like India.

SPML INFRA: ICRA UPGRADES CREDIT RATING TO BBB (STABLE) ⭐📈

• ⭐ ICRA upgraded SPML Infra's long-term credit rating to [ICRA]BBB (Stable) from [ICRA]BBB- (Stable)

• 🏦 ₹380 crore term loan and ₹200 crore cash credit facilities were upgraded to [ICRA]BBB (Stable)

• 💼 ₹1,000 crore non-fund based facilities were upgraded to [ICRA]BBB (Stable)/[ICRA]A3+, with the rating also assigned for the enhanced amount

• 📊 ₹15 crore proposed fund-based facilities were also upgraded to [ICRA]BBB (Stable)

• 💰 The rating actions cover total bank facilities of ₹1,595 crore, reflecting an improved credit profile 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The credit rating upgrade enhances SPML Infra's borrowing profile, improves lender confidence and supports future funding.)

The Zee promoter family is on a continuous aim to increase their shareholding in Zee Entertainment by utilizing offshore Mauritius entities.

Here is a quick breakdown of how this has been playing out:

 *​What is Sunbright?* 
It is a Mauritius-based investment vehicle owned by the Subhash Chandra/Goenka promoter family. It used to be called Essel Holdings but was rebranded to Sunbright to distance it from past debt issues.

 *​The Sony Merger Connection:* 
When the Zee-Sony merger was being planned, Sunbright was the exact entity set up to receive shares and non-compete fees from Sony. The goal was to help the family infuse money back into the merged company to keep their ownership stake. That fell through when the merger was cancelled.

​ *Past Shareholder Rejection & FCCBs:* 
They actually tried a similar fundraise via Sunbright last year to pump 2,237 crore Rupees into Zee. However, institutional shareholders banded together and voted it down because they disapproved of the pricing. Following that rejection and the withdrawal of the fundraise, Zee had to look elsewhere for cash and ended up issuing 239 million dollars in Foreign Currency Convertible Bonds (FCCBs) to raise immediate funds.

Follow the Beat The Street | Stock Market Insights channel on WhatsApp: https://whatsapp.com/channel/0029Vb8R9fH47XeEgsFs7T2s

 *​What is happening now:* 
Now they are pushing again. The Zee Board just approved a fresh, massive plan to issue 24.95 crore warrants to Sunbright worth over 3,143 crore Rupees at 126 Rupees per share. If it clears, the promoter family's ownership stake will jump from just 4% to around 20-24%.

​Basically, they are using the same offshore entity from the failed Sony deal to try and regain control of Zee. It still faces the major hurdle of getting shareholder approval, which tripped them up last time.

GNG Electronics Strengthens India's Refurbished ICT Play with Redington Partnership

Redington is India's largest IT products distributor with a deep network across Tier 1/2/3 cities, enterprise customers, education institutions and resellers.

Instead of building this network organically over years, GNG gets immediate access to one of India's strongest ICT distribution ecosystems.

India's refurbished laptop market remains largely fragmented and unorganized.

By partnering with a trusted distributor like Redington:
=> Refurbished products become easier for enterprises to procure.
=> Electronics Bazaar gets institutional credibility.
=> Corporate buyers gain confidence due to warranty-backed products.

The company is not solely relying on direct sakes or online marketplaces, but they are strengthening distributor network, reseller ecosystem and system integrators.

The partnership enhances GNG Electronics' distribution capabilities, strengthens its position in the organized refurbished ICT market, and can accelerate domestic growth without significant incremental investment in sales infrastructure

The Supreme Court has issued a stern warning on the use of AI in courts.

In a landmark ruling, it struck down an NCLT order after finding reliance on fake and AI-generated legal precedents, comparing such hallucinated judgments to a "methyl isocyanate leak" in the justice system. The court has adopted a zero-tolerance stance and stressed that AI can assist, but human judgment must remain in control.

• 🏦 The proceeds will be primarily used to substantially deleverage the balance sheet, strengthen financial position and fund the company's next phase of growth

• 🌐 The company said the strong investor response reflects confidence in its optical connectivity business and growth opportunities driven by AI data centres, telecom and digital infrastructure 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The successful ₹1,500 crore QIP strengthens STL's balance sheet, reduces leverage and provides capital to support future growth.)

SIYARAM RECYCLING: Receives ₹97.70 lakh brass scrap order from Metal Scrap India.

BANK OF BARODA: Reports strong Q1 growth with global business up 15.46% YoY.

Bright Outdoor Media Limited Expands Its Dominance in the Mira–Bhayandar Growth Corridor with Another Landmark Hoarding at Western Express Highway

The Company has expanded its premium advertising inventory with the addition of a new hoarding at the fast-growing Mira–Bhayandar corridor, further strengthening its presence across one of the most strategic transit destinations in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region.

Key Highlights:
- New hoarding measures 30 ft. × 30 ft. on both Front & Back faces, adding 1,800 sq. ft. of premium advertising inventory
- Follows the recent launch of two 40 ft. × 30 ft. hoardings at the same location, contributing 4,800 sq. ft.
- Total premium advertising inventory at the Mira–Bhayandar corridor now stands at 6,600 sq. ft.
- Advertisers can now book a set of three back-to-back hoardings, creating an unmatched road-blocking impact across the entire stretch
- Strategically located on the key corridor connecting Mumbai with Ahmedabad, Surat, Vadodara, Virar, Vasai, Thane and the rapidly developing Ghodbunder Road corridor
- Delivers exceptional visibility with repeated exposure to lakhs of daily commuters
- Ideal for real estate, automotive, FMCG, retail, entertainment, finance and luxury brands
- Continues to strengthen Bright's portfolio of premium, high-visibility outdoor advertising assets across key transit corridors

SEAMEC: VESSEL 'SEAMEC PALADIN' RETURNS TO INDIA, ONGC DEPLOYMENT TO RESUME 🚢🇮🇳

• 🇮🇳 SEAMEC PALADIN arrived in India on 2 July 2026 after being held up in Dubai following dry docking due to geopolitical developments

• ⏳ After completing necessary formalities, the vessel is expected to be redeployed with ONGC under its long-term charter in around 15 days

• ⚓ The return marks the end of the vessel's operational disruption caused by the geopolitical situation

• 🤝 Redeployment with ONGC will enable the vessel to resume contracted offshore operations

• 📋 The company disclosed the vessel's arrival and expected redeployment timeline under SEBI LODR Regulation 30 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The vessel's return and planned redeployment with ONGC restores revenue-generating operations under its long-term charter.)

HFCL: CARE UPGRADES CREDIT RATINGS OF MATERIAL SUBSIDIARY HTL ⭐📈

• ⭐ CARE Ratings upgraded the credit ratings of HTL Limited, HFCL's material subsidiary, across multiple bank facilities and assigned a Positive Outlook

• 📊 Long-term bank facilities were upgraded from CARE BBB+; Stable to CARE A (CE); Positive, while short-term facilities were upgraded from CARE A2 to CARE A1 (CE)

• 🏦 CARE also assigned a CARE A-; Positive / CARE A2+ rating to ₹230 crore of long-term/short-term bank facilities

• 📈 The rating upgrade reflects improved operational and financial performance and a strengthened credit profile of HTL Limited

• 🤝 The improved ratings are expected to enhance lender confidence and reinforce HTL's business prospects and financial position 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The credit rating upgrade strengthens HTL's borrowing profile and reflects improving business fundamentals, which is positive for HFCL.)

BLS E-SERVICES: ACQUISITION OF ATYATI TO BOOST AI-DRIVEN BANKING SOLUTIONS 🤖🏦

• 🤝 Successfully completed the 100% acquisition of Atyati Technologies in an all-cash deal of around ₹157 crore

• 🏦 Atyati serves 35+ banks and financial institutions through 25,900+ Customer Service Points (CSPs) covering around 1 lakh villages across India

• 🤖 The acquisition strengthens BLS E-Services' AI-driven banking technology, financial inclusion capabilities and Business Correspondent (BC) network

• 📈 The company expects the acquisition to generate significant synergies through network expansion, enhanced scale and improved cost efficiencies, supporting long-term growth and margins

• 🌐 The deal enhances BLS E-Services' ability to deliver AI-powered banking solutions, micro-lending platforms and last-mile banking infrastructure nationwide 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The acquisition significantly strengthens BLS E-Services' technology capabilities, expands its banking network and supports long-term growth in financial inclusion and AI-driven banking.)

HAPPY SQUARE (WHITE FORCE): WINS ₹4.5 CR WORK ORDER EXTENSION 📑💼

• 🤝 Received a one-year extension of its existing manpower supply services work order from BRIC–National Agri-Food and Biomanufacturing Institute (NABI)

• 💰 The extended contract is valued at approximately ₹4.50 crore (inclusive of all taxes)

• 📅 The original contract, awarded in June 2024, has been extended on the same terms and conditions until 29 July 2027

• 🇮🇳 The order has been awarded by a domestic entity, with no promoter interest and no related-party transaction

• 📈 The company expects the extension to generate around ₹4.50 crore in revenue and strengthen its order book 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The contract extension provides revenue visibility, strengthens the order book and supports business growth.)

BIKEWO GREEN TECH: SIGNS MOU TO ACQUIRE 51% IN POSITIVEV MOBILITY 🚗⚡

• 🤝 Signed a strategic MoU with PositiEV Mobility Pvt. Ltd. to acquire a 51% stake, subject to due diligence, valuation, definitive agreements and regulatory approvals

• 👨‍💼 Upon completion of the proposed transaction, Hiten Pal Saklani, Founder of PositiEV Mobility, is proposed to be appointed as CEO of BikeWo, subject to Board approval

• 🔋 The acquisition aims to build an integrated EV ecosystem covering EV retail, leasing, financing, charging infrastructure, battery swapping, fleet solutions and after-sales services

• 🌐 The partnership will provide BikeWo access to PositiEV's dealer network, institutional customers, financiers and strategic partners, accelerating expansion in commercial EV mobility

• 📈 BikeWo plans to strengthen partnerships with OEMs, financial institutions, charging infrastructure providers and fleet operators to scale its EV business across India 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The proposed acquisition strengthens BikeWo's EV ecosystem, expands its market presence and supports long-term growth in India's electric mobility sector.)

GOENKA BUSINESS & FINANCE: SEBI IMPOSES ₹1 CRORE PENALTY, 5-YEAR MARKET BAN ⚖️🚫

• 🚨 SEBI passed a final order dated 30 June 2026 against 226 entities, including Goenka Business & Finance Ltd.

• 💰 SEBI imposed a monetary penalty of ₹1 crore on the company

• ⛔ The company has been restrained from accessing the securities market and prohibited from buying, selling or dealing in securities for 5 years

• 📅 The order was received on 1 July 2026, and the company said it reserves the right to appeal before the Securities Appellate Tribunal (SAT) and, if required, the Supreme Court of India

• 📉 The company acknowledged that the SEBI order is expected to have an adverse financial impact 

🔴 Impact: Negative (The ₹1 crore penalty and 5-year securities market ban are significant regulatory actions that could materially affect the company's operations and market standing.)

3B FILMS: APPROVES ₹100 CR RIGHTS ISSUE & CAPITAL HIKE 💰📈

• 💵 Board approved a Rights Issue of equity shares to raise up to ₹100 crore, with detailed terms including issue price and entitlement ratio to be decided later

• 🏢 Constituted a Rights Issue Committee to oversee the fund raise and approved the Draft Letter of Offer (DLOF) along with the appointment of intermediaries

• 📈 Approved an increase in authorised share capital from ₹52 crore to ₹125 crore, subject to shareholders' approval

• 📅 The company fixed the 12th AGM on 8 August 2026 and approved the related AGM notice and Directors' Report

• 📋 The Rights Issue remains subject to regulatory approvals, with the number of shares to be determined after receiving in-principle approval from the stock exchange 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The proposed ₹100 crore Rights Issue and capital expansion are expected to strengthen the company's financial position and support future growth plans.)

BIZOTIC COMMERCIAL: APPROVES 5:1 BONUS ISSUE & AUTHORIZED CAPITAL HIKE 🎁📈

• 🎁 Board approved a bonus issue in the ratio of 5:1, with shareholders to receive 5 bonus shares for every 1 existing equity share, subject to shareholders' approval

• 📈 Approved an increase in authorised share capital from ₹11.27 crore to ₹57.86 crore to facilitate the bonus issue

• 💰 The company proposes to issue 4,82,10,000 bonus equity shares, capitalising up to ₹48.21 crore from securities premium and/or free reserves

• 📅 The record date for the bonus issue will be decided by the Board, while the 10th AGM is scheduled for 1 August 2026 to seek shareholders' approval

• 👨‍💼 The Board also approved the appointment of M/s. Shweta Jain & Co LLP as Statutory Auditors, M/s. Sarang Shivajirao Chavan & Associates as Internal Auditors, and noted the resignation of an Independent Director 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The 5:1 bonus issue is a major shareholder-friendly move that enhances liquidity and reflects management's confidence in the company's financial position.)

PREMIER ENERGIES: TO INCORPORATE WOS FOR BATTERY STORAGE BUSINESS 🔋🏭

• 🏢 Board approved the incorporation of a wholly owned subsidiary with an authorised share capital of ₹10 lakh

• 🔋 The new subsidiary will focus on Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS), battery cells, battery materials, and related electronics, hardware and software businesses

• 💰 Premier Energies will subscribe to 50,000 equity shares of ₹10 each, amounting to an initial investment of ₹5 lakh

• 📋 The name of the subsidiary will be finalized after approval by the Central Registration Centre, Ministry of Corporate Affairs

• 📢 The company said detailed disclosures under SEBI LODR will be made in due course 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The new subsidiary strengthens Premier Energies' expansion into the fast-growing battery storage and energy solutions segment.)

EQUITAS SFB: Q1 ADVANCES UP 27%, DISBURSEMENTS SURGE 94% 🏦📈

• 💰 Gross advances grew 26.7% YoY and 3.2% QoQ to ₹47,653 crore, driven by robust lending

• 🚀 Disbursements surged 93.8% YoY to around ₹6,804 crore, though they declined 7.4% QoQ

• 📊 Total deposits increased 10.4% YoY and 5.3% QoQ to ₹48,976 crore

• 🌾 Micro Finance & Micro Loans rose 70.2% YoY to ₹6,019 crore, while Non-Micro loans grew 22.2% YoY to ₹41,634 crore

• 🏦 CASA ratio stood at 25%, while the Credit-Deposit (CD) ratio was 92.96% as of 30 June 2026

BROOKS LABORATORIES: INCORPORATES WHOLLY OWNED SUBSIDIARY 💊🏢

• 🏢 Incorporated a wholly owned subsidiary, Abridge Speciality Limited, on 2 July 2026, with the Ministry of Corporate Affairs issuing the Certificate of Incorporation on the same day

• 💰 The new subsidiary has an authorised share capital of ₹1 lakh, with Brooks Laboratories holding 100% ownership

• 💊 The subsidiary will engage in the manufacturing and marketing of pharmaceuticals and related products

• 📈 The incorporation aligns with the company's strategic objective to expand its business and strengthen its pharmaceutical operations

• 🚀 As a newly incorporated entity, Abridge Speciality Limited is yet to commence business operations and has no historical turnover 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The incorporation supports Brooks Laboratories' expansion strategy and creates a platform for future business growth.)

HARISH TEXTILE: RECEIVES NOTICE TO VACATE FACTORY PREMISES 🏭⚠️

• 📩 Received two communications from Harish Enterprise Pvt. Ltd., the licensor of one of its factory premises at Umbergaon, Gujarat, directing the company to vacate the premises

• 📅 The licensor stated that the Leave and License Agreements expired on 30 June 2026 and asked the company to hand over vacant possession within 15 days of receipt of the notice

• ⚖️ The notice states that appropriate legal action may be initiated if the company fails to vacate the premises within the stipulated period

• 🛡️ The company clarified that no violation or contravention has been alleged against it and is reviewing the matter with its legal advisors

• 📊 The company said the financial and operational impact cannot be quantified at present 

🔴 Impact: Negative (The notice could disrupt operations at the affected facility if the issue is not resolved, creating operational uncertainty.)

BLS E-SERVICES: COMPLETES ₹156.82 CR ACQUISITION OF ATYATI TECHNOLOGIES 🤝💼

• ✅ Successfully completed the 100% acquisition of Atyati Technologies Pvt. Ltd. on 2 July 2026, making it a wholly owned subsidiary

• 💰 The acquisition was completed for ₹156.82 crore through cash consideration, subject to certain conditions

• 🏦 Atyati is a Business Correspondent (BC) and technology company providing financial inclusion, micro-lending and banking technology solutions, with a network across 1 lakh villages

• 📈 The acquisition will help BLS E-Services expand and consolidate its BC business, while strengthening its presence in the financial inclusion ecosystem

• 💼 Atyati reported FY26 revenue from operations of ₹375.8 crore 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The acquisition strengthens BLS E-Services' financial inclusion platform, expands its BC business and enhances long-term growth opportunities.)

PRIMO CHEMICALS: TO ACQUIRE REMAINING 51% STAKE IN FLOW TECH 🏭🤝

• 🏢 Board approved the acquisition of the remaining 51% equity stake in Flow Tech Chemicals Pvt. Ltd., making it a 100% wholly owned subsidiary, subject to shareholders' approval

• 💰 The acquisition will be completed through cash consideration at ₹1,418.20 per share, with completion targeted by 31 March 2027

• 📈 Flow Tech reported FY26 turnover of ₹341.66 crore and PAT of ₹9.80 crore, and manufactures Chlorinated Paraffin and Hydrochloric Acid

• ⚙️ The acquisition is expected to enhance operational control, strengthen business synergies, improve chlorine utilization and generate long-term operational and financial benefits

• 🗳️ The Board also approved a postal ballot to seek shareholders' approval for the acquisition and other resolutions 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The acquisition will strengthen Primo Chemicals' integration, improve operational efficiencies and enhance long-term growth prospects.)

ASSTON PHARMACEUTICALS: TO RAISE ₹27.72 CR VIA PREFERENTIAL ISSUE 💰📈

• 💵 Board approved a preferential issue of up to 24,10,431 equity shares at ₹115 per share (₹10 face value + ₹105 premium)

• 💰 The issue will raise up to ₹27.72 crore from non-promoter investors, subject to shareholders' and regulatory approvals

• 📅 The company has convened an EGM on 31 July 2026 to seek shareholders' approval for the preferential issue

• 👥 Major proposed allottees include Vijay Rathee (13.17 lakh shares) and Vijaylaxmi Infra Projects Pvt. Ltd. (7.83 lakh shares)

• 📈 The funds are proposed to be raised entirely through cash consideration via the preferential allotment route 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The proposed fund raise is expected to strengthen the company's capital base and support future growth initiatives.)

TATA MOTORS PV

JLR Q1 SALES DATA

Retail sales at 80,000 units, down 15.3% YoY

Wholesale volumes at 79,300 units, down 9.2% YoY

Wholesale volumes down 16.8% QoQ, retail sales down 13.8% QoQ

JLR SAYS

Q1 volumes affected by temporary supply constraints, disruption by war

Parle Products, maker of iconic Parle-G, eyeing $1bn plus IPO at $10 bn plus valuation; picks advisors

https://www.moneycontrol.com/news/business/ipo/parle-products-maker-of-iconic-parle-g-eyeing-1bn-plus-ipo-at-10-bn-plus-valuation-picks-advisors-13964686.html

TITAGARH RAIL: PARTNERS IIT MADRAS-INCUBATED TUTR HYPERLOOP FOR FREIGHT SOLUTIONS 🚄🤝

• 🤝 Signed a strategic collaboration agreement with TuTr Hyperloop, an IIT Madras-incubated deep-tech startup, to develop India's first Hyperloop-enabled freight mobility solutions

• 🚚 The partnership will design, develop, integrate and explore commercialization of high-speed, energy-efficient Hyperloop-based cargo transportation systems

• 🇮🇳 The collaboration aims to build indigenous Hyperloop technology, supporting the Make in India, Aatmanirbhar Bharat and Viksit Bharat initiatives

• 🌍 The companies will evaluate cargo and freight transportation opportunities for both domestic and international markets

• ⚙️ The partnership combines Titagarh Rail's rolling stock manufacturing expertise with TuTr Hyperloop's propulsion, control systems and Hyperloop technology capabilities 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The strategic collaboration strengthens Titagarh Rail's innovation pipeline and positions it in next-generation freight mobility technologies.)

ABFRL: NCLT APPROVES MERGER OF JAYPORE & TG APPAREL INTO ABFRL ⚖️👔

• ✅ NCLT, Mumbai Bench has sanctioned the Scheme of Amalgamation of Jaypore E-Commerce Pvt. Ltd. and TG Apparel & Decor Pvt. Ltd. with Aditya Birla Fashion and Retail Ltd. (ABFRL)

• 📅 The appointed date for the merger is 1 April 2026

• 🏢 Both Jaypore E-Commerce and TG Apparel & Decor are wholly owned subsidiaries of ABFRL

• 🔄 The merger aims to simplify the corporate structure, streamline operations, reduce administrative costs and improve financial flexibility

• 📋 No new shares or cash consideration will be issued as part of the amalgamation, since both entities are wholly owned subsidiaries 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The merger simplifies ABFRL's corporate structure, improves operational efficiency and supports long-term value creation.)

GSP CROP SCIENCE: APPROVES INTERNAL RESTRUCTURING SCHEME 🌱🔄

• 🏢 Board approved a Scheme of Arrangement involving the amalgamation of Rajdhani Petrochemicals Pvt. Ltd. and the demerger of the manufacturing undertaking of GSP Intermediates Pvt. Ltd. into GSP Crop Science Ltd., subject to regulatory approvals

• 📅 The appointed date for the scheme is 1 April 2026

• 🔄 The restructuring aims to streamline the corporate structure, consolidate assets and liabilities, improve operational synergies and reduce compliance costs

• 💰 The transaction involves no cash consideration, and no new shares will be issued, as both entities are wholly owned subsidiaries of GSP Crop Science

• 📊 The shareholding pattern of GSP Crop Science will remain unchanged after the scheme becomes effective 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The internal restructuring is expected to improve operational efficiency, simplify the corporate structure and support long-term growth without shareholder dilution.)

US JUN AVERAGE WORKWEEK UNCHANGED AT 34.3 HOURS

US JUN LABOR-FORCE PARTICIPATION RATE 61.5%

US MAY PAYROLLS REVISED TO +129K; APR REVISED TO +148K

US MAY UNEMPLOYMENT UNREVISED AT 4.3%

US LABOR JUN NONFARM PAYROLLS +57K; CONSENSUS +115K

US JUN AVERAGE HOURLY EARNINGS +0.35%, OR +$0.13 TO $37.64; OVER YEAR +3.52%

US JUN PRIVATE SECTOR PAYROLLS +49K AND GOVERNMENT PAYROLLS +8K

UJJIVAN SMALL FINANCE BANK: Q1 DEPOSITS UP 25%, LOAN BOOK GROWS 29% 📈🏦

• 💰 Total deposits rose 25.1% YoY to ₹48,307 crore, while the gross loan book grew 28.9% YoY to ₹42,903 crore

• 🚀 Disbursements increased 41.5% YoY to ₹9,252 crore, led by strong growth in Gold Loans (+184.5%) and FIG (+185.2%)

• 🏡 Secured loan book jumped 42.2% YoY to ₹21,555 crore, with the secured portfolio rising to 50.2% of the total loan book

• 📉 Gross NPA improved to 2.17% from 2.52% a year ago, while Collection Efficiency remained robust at 99.68%

• 🏦 CASA deposits grew 37.8% YoY to ₹12,930 crore, with a CASA ratio of 26.8%

SHAHI SHIPPING: BOARD APPROVES SALE OF MOVABLE ASSETS 🚢📦

• 🏢 Board of Directors approved the sale/disposal of the company's movable assets

• 💰 The assets will be sold at the best available market price

• 📅 The decision was approved at the Board meeting held on 2 July 2026

• 📋 The disclosure has been made under SEBI LODR Regulation 30

• 🔄 The company has not disclosed the value of assets or the expected financial impact of the sale 

🟡 Impact: Neutral (The disclosure provides limited details, and the financial impact cannot be assessed until further information is available.)

PC JEWELLER: Q1 REVENUE UP 21%, TO BECOME DEBT-FREE THIS QUARTER 💎📈

• 💰 Consolidated revenue grew approximately 21% YoY in Q1 FY27, reflecting continued operational improvement

• 📉 Reduced outstanding bank debt by another ~24% during Q1 FY27

• 🏦 The company has now reduced over 90% of its outstanding debt since the Joint Settlement Agreement signed in September 2024

• ✅ Management expects to achieve debt-free status in the current quarter, which is expected to significantly strengthen its financial position

• 📋 The business update is provisional and subject to limited review by the statutory auditor

INDIA GLYCOLS: IGL SPIRITS APPOINTS MANOJ KUMAR RAI AS COO 🥃👔

• 👨‍💼 IGL Spirits Ltd., a wholly owned subsidiary of India Glycols Ltd., has appointed Manoj Kumar Rai as its Chief Operating Officer (COO)

• 📅 The appointment is effective from 2 July 2026

• 🏭 Manoj Kumar Rai brings over 27 years of experience across the engineering, consumer goods, entertainment and alcoholic beverages sectors

• 🥃 He previously served as Chief Revenue Officer at Allied Blenders & Distillers and has also held leadership roles at Pernod Ricard India, Marico Industries and Saregama India

• 🎓 He holds a PGDM from IIM Lucknow and a B.Tech from IIT Delhi 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The appointment of an experienced industry executive is expected to strengthen IGL Spirits' leadership and support its growth strategy.)

MARUTI SUZUKI: INAUGURATES ₹35,000 CR KHARKHODA MEGA PLANT 🚗🏭

• 🏭 Inaugurated its Kharkhoda vehicle manufacturing facility in Haryana, spread across 800 acres, by Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Japan PM Sanae Takaichi

• 🚘 The plant has an initial capacity of 5 lakh vehicles per year, scalable to 10 lakh units annually, with a total investment of ₹35,000 crore

• 🌍 At full scale, the facility will be among the world's largest vehicle manufacturing plants, supporting Maruti Suzuki's 4 million units annual production capacity ambition

• 🌱 The smart factory will run on 100% renewable electricity, with solar power, biogas, battery energy storage, and zero liquid discharge systems

• 👨‍🏭 The project is expected to create over 21,000 jobs and strengthen the India–Japan manufacturing partnership 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The mega investment significantly expands Maruti Suzuki's manufacturing capacity, strengthens its long-term growth prospects and reinforces its leadership in the Indian automobile market.)

DMART: Q1 REVENUE RISES 15.1% YoY TO ₹18,343 CR 🛒📈

• 💰 Standalone revenue from operations increased 15.1% YoY to ₹18,343.49 crore in Q1 FY27

ADANI ENTERPRISES: FORMS 50:50 JV WITH IHC'S IRH FOR $11.5 BN ALUMINIUM PROJECT 🏭🤝

• 🤝 Adani Enterprises and International Resources Holding (IRH), an IHC Group company, will form a 50:50 joint venture to develop an integrated greenfield aluminium project in Odisha

• 💰 The project involves an investment of USD 11.5 billion (≈₹1.08 lakh crore), making it Odisha's largest FDI proposal and India's largest FDI in the metallurgy sector

• 🏗️ The project includes a 4 MMTPA alumina refinery, 2 MMTPA aluminium smelter, 4,000 MW captive power plant and 1 MMTPA downstream manufacturing park

• 📅 The project will be developed in two phases with investments of ₹66,000 crore and ₹44,000 crore, and is expected to generate around 53,500 jobs

• 🌍 The investment aims to position Odisha as a global aluminium manufacturing hub and strengthen India's role in the global aluminium supply chain 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The mega JV significantly strengthens Adani Enterprises' presence in the metals sector, drives long-term growth and reinforces India's manufacturing ecosystem.)

MARUTI SUZUKI: 

CO KHARKHODA PLANT INAUGURATED BY PM MODI & JAPAN PM SANAE TAKAICHI 

CURRENT CAPACITY 0.5M UNITS, SCALABLE TO 1M 

TOTAL INVESTMENT ₹35,000 CR 

FACILITY TO RUN ON 100% RENEWABLE ELECTRICITY

PC Jeweller's turnaround continues to gather pace.

The company reported a ~21% YoY growth in consolidated revenue for Q1 FY27.

But the bigger highlight is on the balance sheet.

 - Outstanding debt has been reduced by 90%+ since the September 2024 settlement with banks.
 - Another 24% reduction was achieved during Q1 FY27.
 - Management expects the company to become completely debt-free in the current quarter.

From debt restructuring to nearly debt-free in less than two years—it's been a remarkable turnaround.

Now the key question is whether the company can sustain this operational momentum after cleaning up its balance sheet.

O-P, TP cut to Rs 3500 from Rs 5000

While Zudio has overshadowed Westside in store adds over last 5+ years, Westside is still 40% of Trent’s total retail area & surprised on store

additions in F26 after lackluster F23-F25 years

Proprietary store data indicates 3 things for Westside

(1) Store rationalization is over and helped Trent move away from certain cities & markets,

SEBI Cracks ₹144 Crore Stock Pump-and-Dump Scam Using Digital Trail

India’s market regulator uncovered a ₹143.79 crore pump-and-dump racket involving 226 entities across five listed companies.

The investigation relied on extensive digital evidence, including airline booking records, WhatsApp chats, food delivery orders, call records, bank transactions, and location data to establish links between the operators.

SEBI imposed ₹47.8 crore in monetary penalties and barred the accused from the securities market for multiple years.

The case underscores SEBI’s growing use of digital forensics and data analytics to trace coordinated market manipulation and insider networks.

Atharva Polyplast looks more than just a plastic component maker.
What stands out is its end-to-end manufacturing setup — from design and tooling to moulding, finishing, and assembly — all under one roof.

A few things make the story interesting:

Strong compliance stack helps it qualify for global customers
Margins have improved sharply, driven by a better product mix
Engineering services can become a meaningful third growth engine
The company has scale-up headroom, backed by land, capacity, and premium market opportunity

In simple words, Atharva seems to be positioning itself as a precision manufacturing partner for global players, not just a commodity supplier.

If execution stays on track, this could be a business worth tracking closely.

**Prestige Estates – Morgan Stanley View**

Morgan Stanley remains bullish on Prestige Estates, citing strong pre-sales momentum, resilient demand, growing leasing income and attractive embedded value in its rental portfolio.

**Brokerage View**
- Rating: Overweight
- Target price: ₹1,920
- Positive long-term outlook
- Strong execution continues

**Key Takeaways**
- Strong start to 1QFY27
- Pre-sales: ₹6,300 crore in first two months
- Achieved ~18% of FY27 estimate
- Ahead of expected run-rate

**Demand Outlook**
- Demand remains healthy
- Stable footfalls and conversions
- ASPs remain resilient
- No meaningful AI-related demand impact in Bengaluru or Hyderabad

**Leasing Update**
- Tower X, BKC: ~70% leased
- Mahalakshmi project: ~10% leased
- Leasing rate around ₹400/sq. ft.
- Commercial portfolio gaining traction

**Hospitality Business**
- Hospitality listing may be deferred
- Awaiting completion of Aerocity hotel
- Aerocity project to have 778 keys
- Listing could unlock value later

**Rental Potential**
- FY29 rental income potential: ₹4,000 crore
- Assumed cap rate: 7.5%
- Rental portfolio worth nearly two-thirds of Enterprise Value
- Highlights undervalued residential and hospitality businesses

**Why It Matters**
- Strong pre-sales momentum
- Healthy commercial leasing
- Significant rental income growth
- Attractive valuation support

**Key Takeaway**
- Morgan Stanley believes **Prestige Estates is well-positioned for sustained growth, supported by strong residential demand, expanding commercial leasing, significant rental income potential and hidden value in its hospitality and residential businesses, while maintaining an Overweight rating with a target price of ₹1,920.**

__Disclaimer: I am not a SEBI-registered Analyst. This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

**Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs) – CLSA View**

CLSA believes the recent sharp fall in crude oil prices provides near-term earnings relief for India's oil marketing companies, but cautions against extrapolating these gains over the long term.

**Sector View**

- Lower crude boosts marketing margins
- Earnings recovery expected
- Long-term risks remain

- Sustainable only if Brent remains around US$70–75/bbl

- Limited upside if oil prices rise

- Near-term earnings supportive
- Crude below US$70/bbl is ideal
- Policy decisions remain key
- Monitor fuel taxation closely

**Why It Matters**
- Lower crude boosts profitability
- Excise policy is a major variable
- Margin sustainability uncertain
- Oil prices remain key driver

**Key Takeaway**
- CLSA believes **the recent fall in crude prices offers a welcome earnings recovery for IOC, BPCL and HPCL through stronger fuel marketing margins. However, investors should remain cautious, as any reversal of the ₹10/litre excise duty cut or a rebound in Brent crude above US$70–75/bbl could significantly reduce the sector's long-term upside.**

ABS Marine Services Secures ~ ₹ 126.12 Cr Long-Term Charter Contract for Offshore Support Vessel MV ARTEMIS

ABS Marine Services Limited, one of the leading maritime companies offering comprehensive services in Vessel Ownership, Ship Management, Marine and Port Services has signed a Charter Party Agreement with Hardy Exploration & Production (India) Inc., acting on behalf of itself and its joint venture partners, Oil and Natural Gas Corporation Limited (ONGC) and Invenire Petrodyne Limited, for the deployment of its Anchor Handling Tug Supply (AHTS) Vessel, MV ARTEMIS.

Under the agreement, MV ARTEMIS will be deployed to support petroleum exploration and production activities on the east coast of India. The contract has a firm charter tenure of 1,825 days (5 years) from the date of mobilisation, with an option for extension by up to a further 1,825 days at the charterer's discretion. The contract is valued at approximately ₹126.12 Cr (inclusive of GST) over the firm charter period.

Strategic Impact
The contract reinforces the company’s position as a trusted offshore service provider and enhances revenue visibility through a long-term charter arrangement. The deployment of MV ARTEMIS under a multi-year contract is expected to support stable vessel utilization and strengthen the Company's offshore operations portfolio.

The award also reflects the Company's proven operational capabilities and strong relationships within the offshore energy sector. With increasing focus on domestic exploration and offshore energy development, the company remains well positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities and further expand its presence in the offshore support services segment.

**YES Securities: Banking Sector Top Picks (June 2026)**

**Top Preference Banks**
- Bank of Baroda – BUY | TP: ₹400
- HDFC Bank – BUY | TP: ₹1,125
- Kotak Mahindra Bank – BUY | TP: ₹525
- Axis Bank – BUY | TP: ₹1,750
- ICICI Bank – BUY | TP: ₹1,750
- State Bank of India – BUY | TP: ₹1,300

**Other Preferred Banks**
- Bank of Maharashtra – BUY | TP: ₹110
- DCB Bank – BUY | TP: ₹230
- CSB Bank – BUY | TP: ₹400
- Indian Bank – BUY | TP: ₹1,025
- IndusInd Bank – BUY | TP: ₹1,125
- Federal Bank – BUY | TP: ₹395
- City Union Bank – BUY | TP: ₹240
- Karur Vysya Bank – BUY | TP: ₹350

**ADD Rated Banks**
- RBL Bank – ADD | TP: ₹425
- IDFC First Bank – ADD | TP: ₹90

**Investment Themes**
- Corporate credit revival
- Stable to improving NIMs
- Strong CASA franchises
- Healthy loan growth
- Improving asset quality
- Controlled credit costs
- PAT recovery in FY27–FY29

**Highest Conviction Large Banks**
- Bank of Baroda
- HDFC Bank
- Kotak Mahindra Bank
- Axis Bank
- ICICI Bank
- State Bank of India

**Preferred Mid & Small Banks**
- Bank of Maharashtra
- Indian Bank
- Federal Bank
- DCB Bank
- CSB Bank
- Karur Vysya Bank

**Why It Matters**
- Earnings recovery expected
- Loan growth remains healthy
- Strong capital adequacy
- Better corporate lending outlook
- Attractive valuations across banks
- Improving profitability trajectory

**Key Takeaway**
- YES Securities expects the **banking sector to witness a strong earnings recovery over FY27–FY29**, supported by **healthy credit growth, stable margins, improving asset quality and controlled credit costs**. Its highest-conviction picks include **Bank of Baroda, HDFC Bank, Kotak Mahindra Bank, Axis Bank, ICICI Bank and SBI**, while select mid-sized banks such as **Indian Bank, Federal Bank and Bank of Maharashtra** offer additional growth opportunities.

**Swing trading | Positional Trading

Trent’s scale up has been exceptional under Noel Tata’s leadership.

From FY15-26:
- REV increased from 1,242 Cr to 19,701 Cr
- PAT increased from 54 Cr to 1,987 Cr
- Stores increased from 80 to 1,282

Store expansion roadmap is very aggressive:
- Westside - 50 stores every year
- Zudio - 200-250 stores every year
- Star Bazaar - 25-40 stores every year

Long term store ambition
- Westside - 700 stores vs around 300 currently
- Zudio - 5,000 stores vs around 963 currently

**InCred on Aether Industries**

🟡 **Rating:** Hold  
🎯 **Target Price:** ₹1,140

**Key Positives**
- Strong specialty chemicals franchise
- High-entry-barrier product portfolio
- Deep process know-how
- Sticky customer relationships

**Growth Drivers**
- Stable pharmaceutical intermediates business
- New product platforms
- Business diversification
- Specialty chemistry focus

**Brokerage View**
- Valuation remains expensive
- High capital expenditure
- Return ratios still subdued
- Near-term upside limited

**Why It Matters**
- Strong long-term business quality
- Execution remains healthy
- Capital efficiency needs improvement
- Valuation caps re-rating potential

**Key Takeaway**
- InCred has **downgraded Aether Industries to Hold** with a **₹1,140 target price**. While the brokerage continues to view Aether as a **high-quality specialty chemicals company** with strong competitive advantages and diversified growth opportunities, it believes the **current valuation, elevated capex, and modest return ratios** limit near-term upside.

**1100++ Concall Highlights for Q4FY26
****@Concalls3**

**Investec on Vedanta**

🟢 **Rating:** Buy  
🎯 **Target Price:** ₹319

**Analyst Day Highlights**
- Roadmap shared for Zinc International
- Saudi copper strategy outlined
- Gamsberg turnaround initiatives
- Long-term portfolio expansion

**Key Growth Drivers**
- Saudi copper mine opportunity
- Smelter IRR target of 16%
- Operational improvement initiatives
- Global base metals expansion

**Brokerage View**
- Gamsberg execution remains key
- Ore grade challenges persist
- Blending remains current solution
- Awaiting detailed mine development plans

**Why It Matters**
- Strengthens international mining portfolio
- Saudi assets offer long-term growth
- Better execution can improve profitability
- Further upside depends on project clarity

**Key Takeaway**
- Investec maintains a **Buy** rating on **Vedanta** with a **₹319 target price**. The brokerage remains positive on the company's long-term international mining strategy, particularly its **Saudi copper portfolio**, while noting that successful execution at **Gamsberg** and greater clarity on mine development plans will be key drivers for future earnings upgrades.

**1100++ Concall Highlights for Q4FY26
****@Concalls3**

**Kotak Institutional Equities on Escorts Kubota**

🟢 **Rating:** Add  
🎯 **Target Price:** ₹3,400

**Management Highlights**
- Flat domestic tractor industry outlook
- Focus on market share gains
- Non-tractor business expansion
- Construction equipment demand improving

**Growth Drivers**
- New product launches
- Captive finance expansion
- Wider distribution network
- Diversified revenue mix

**Brokerage View**
- Near-term margin pressures persist
- Medium-term profitability levers intact
- Construction equipment recovery encouraging
- Strategy focused on reducing cyclicality

**Why It Matters**
- Market share expansion remains priority
- Diversification lowers earnings volatility
- Improving construction demand supports growth
- Margin recovery could aid future earnings

**Key Takeaway**
- Kotak Institutional Equities maintains an **Add** rating on **Escorts Kubota** with a **₹3,400 target price**. The brokerage remains constructive on the company's strategy to **gain tractor market share, diversify beyond tractors, and benefit from a recovery in construction equipment demand**, while expecting medium-term profitability to improve despite near-term margin headwinds.

🟢 **Rating:** Buy  
🎯 **Target Price:** ₹1,740

- USFDA inspection completed

**Brokerage View**

**Why It Matters**
- Biosimilar pipeline remains intact
- Regulatory progress continues

- Nomura maintains a **Buy** rating on **Dr. Reddy's Laboratories** with a **₹1,740 target price**. While the USFDA issued **seven Form 483 observations** following its Hyderabad biosimilars facility inspection, the brokerage believes most relate to the newer manufacturing block, indicating that earlier contamination concerns have largely been addressed. **Nomura on Persistent Systems**

🟡 **Rating:** Neutral  
🎯 **Target Price:** ₹5,200

**Key Highlights**
- Acquiring 21% stake in Nagarro
- Intends to acquire 100% ownership
- Plans to delist Nagarro
- Strengthens European presence

**Nagarro Snapshot**
- CY25 revenue: €1.0 billion
- 18,500 employees
- Operations across 40+ countries
- Strong digital engineering capabilities

**Business Mix**
- North America: 35%
- Central Europe: 30%
- Rest of Europe: 13%
- Rest of World: 23%

**Financial Profile**
- CY25 EBITDA margin: 13.8%
- CY25 EBIT margin: 8.3%
- Q1 CY26 EBITDA margin: 15.6%
- Q1 CY26 EBIT margin: 12.1%

**Key Takeaway**
- Nomura maintains a **Neutral** rating on Persistent Systems with a **₹5,200 target price**. The brokerage views the proposed **Nagarro acquisition as strategically important**, strengthening Persistent's European footprint and digital engineering capabilities. However, investors are likely to monitor execution, integration, and margin improvement as the company pursues full ownership and delisting of Nagarro.

**Citi on Persistent Systems**

🔴 **Rating:** Sell  
🎯 **Target Price:** ₹4,090

**Key Concerns**
- Nagarro acquisition at 140% premium
- Integration risks to monitor
- Expensive relative valuation
- Combined growth seen in low double digits

**Deal Highlights**
- Offer price: €81 per share
- Implied EV/EBITDA: ~10x CY26
- Strengthens European presence
- Nagarro has ~18,500 employees

**Brokerage View**
- Strategic rationale acknowledged
- Acquisition valuation appears expensive
- Persistent already trades at premium multiples
- Execution and integration remain key monitorables

**Key Takeaway**
- Citi maintains a **Sell** rating on Persistent Systems with a **₹4,090 target price**. While acknowledging the strategic benefits of acquiring Nagarro to strengthen its European footprint, the brokerage believes the acquisition is **priced aggressively** and highlights valuation and integration risks. It expects the deal to be EPS accretive over time but believes the current stock price already reflects optimistic growth expectations.

**CLSA on Persistent Systems**

🟢 **Rating:** High Conviction Outperform  
🎯 **Target Price:** ₹6,520

**Key Positives**
- Acquires European IT firm Nagarro
- Supports FY31 US$5 billion revenue vision
- Attractive acquisition valuation
- Expected EPS accretion of ~6%

**Deal Highlights**
- Enterprise Value: US$1.4 billion
- EV/Sales: 1.2x

- P/E: 13x
- Acquisition at ~140% premium

**Brokerage View**
- Purchase price remains attractive
- Nagarro strengthens digital engineering capabilities
- Expands European presence
- Long-term strategic fit remains strong

**Key Takeaway**
- CLSA maintains a **High Conviction Outperform** rating on Persistent Systems with a **₹6,520 target price**, viewing the **Nagarro acquisition as strategically compelling**. Despite the acquisition premium, the brokerage believes the valuation is attractive given global IT sector derating, expects **around 6% EPS accretion**, and sees the deal as a key milestone toward Persistent's **US$5 billion revenue target by FY31**.

**Investec on Astral**

🟢 **Rating:** Buy  
🎯 **Target Price:** ₹1,710

**Key Positives**
- Board-approved demerger into Astral Chemie
- 1:1 share issuance with mirror shareholding
- Chemicals business to be separately listed
- Creates a pure-play specialty chemicals company

**Growth Drivers**
- Becomes second-largest listed player after Pidilite
- Strong adhesives & construction chemicals portfolio
- Backward integration initiatives
- DSS acquisition supports future growth

**Why It Matters**
- Revenue CAGR of 19% (FY19–26)
- EBITDA CAGR of 18% (FY19–26)
- Healthy cash flow and RoCE profile
- Better business disclosures and valuation discovery

**Key Takeaway**
- Investec maintains a **Buy** rating on Astral with a **₹1,710 target price**, viewing the proposed **Astral Chemie demerger** as a potential value-unlocking catalyst. The brokerage believes the standalone chemicals business, supported by strong growth, healthy cash generation, and strategic initiatives such as backward integration and the DSS acquisition, could attract higher valuation as a focused listed entity.

**Daily Stocks Updates
****@Stockupdate9**

**JPMorgan: Downgrades Pipe Sector**

🔻 **Rating Action**
- Downgrades Astral to Neutral
- Downgrades Supreme Industries to Neutral
- Maintains preference: Astral > Supreme

**Key Reasons**
- Demerger creates capital allocation uncertainty
- Paints & Adhesives business loses plumbing support
- PVC prices expected to decline
- Inventory losses and channel destocking likely

**Astral View**
- Plumbing business may become more aggressive
- Chemicals business growth could slow post demerger
- Valuation impact estimated at only 5–8%
- Long-term view remains constructive

**Supreme View**
- Faces risk from stronger competition in pipes
- Margin expansion outlook becomes challenging
- Near-term growth visibility weakens

**Sector Outlook**
- PVC price correction remains a key overhang
- Tough volume base ahead in Q2
- Better entry points may emerge after volatility subsides

**Key Takeaway**
- JPMorgan has **downgraded Astral and Supreme Industries to Neutral**, citing near-term uncertainty from PVC price correction, inventory losses, and Astral's demerger. While the brokerage remains structurally positive on Astral over the long term, it recommends waiting for improved entry points after near-term volatility eases.

**HDFC Bank: Brokerages Turn Positive**

🟢 **Brokerage Views**

- **Jefferies** – **Buy** | **Target Price: ₹1,050**
- **JPMorgan** – **Overweight** | **Target Price: ₹990**

**Key Positives**
- Independent legal review found no evidence supporting allegations against former Chairman Atanu Chakraborty.
- Governance overhang expected to ease.
- May support appointment of a new Chairman.
- Could improve visibility on CEO Sashidhar Jagdishan's term renewal.

**Additional Positives**
- RBI's FCNR measures may support deposit growth.
- Governance risk premium likely to narrow.
- Valuations remain near multi-year lows.
- Discount to key peer ICICI Bank has widened.

**Why It Matters**
- Reduces governance uncertainty.
- Potential for valuation re-rating.
- Strengthens investor confidence.
- Positive outlook from global brokerages.

**Key Takeaway**
- Both **Jefferies** and **JPMorgan** remain constructive on HDFC Bank following the independent legal review, which helps reduce governance concerns. Combined with attractive valuations, potential benefits from RBI's FCNR initiatives, and the possibility of CEO continuity, the bank could see improved investor sentiment and valuation re-rating.

**Jyoti CNC: Plant Visit Highlights**

**Key Developments**
- Capacity expansion to 16,000 machines
- ₹4,700 crore order book
- Aerospace & defence demand strong
- Huron France capacity expanded

**Growth Drivers**
- Manufacturing automation
- Higher localisation initiatives
- Semiconductor technology development
- Global precision engineering expansion

**Why It Matters**
- 18–24 months revenue visibility
- Aerospace drives 40–45% order book
- EBITDA margin expected above 25%
- Lower import dependence over time

**Future Growth Catalysts**
- Proprietary CNC ecosystem
- Advanced semiconductor equipment
- Operating leverage from expansion
- Increased global manufacturing scale
